sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: CION Investment Corporation (NYSE:CION) Q4 2025 Earnings Call Transcript
feed.summary: CION Investment Corporation (NYSE:CION) reported its Q4 and full-year 2025 earnings, stating a decrease in NAV primarily due to unrealized mark-to-market adjustments in a few equity positions, not credit losses. The company emphasized the strong performance of its core first lien portfolio, representing 81% of investments, and its minimal software exposure. CION also highlighted successful capital market activities, including raising unsecured notes and share repurchases, while maintaining a focus on selective new investments and managing leverage.

feed.title: Zedge (NYSE: ZDGE) posts Q2 2026 growth with record monetization and cash
feed.summary: Zedge (NYSE: ZDGE) reported strong second-quarter fiscal 2026 results, with revenue climbing 18% year-over-year to $8.3 million, driven by significant growth in advertising and subscriptions. Despite a GAAP net loss of $2.3 million due to an intangible asset impairment, the company saw improved non-GAAP net income of $0.8 million and Adjusted EBITDA of $1.1 million, alongside increased free cash flow and a healthy cash balance. Zedge also highlighted progress in its DataSeeds AI training data business and ongoing innovation with new alpha product launches.

feed.title: Carter Bankshares (CARE) CFO covers tax liability with 435 company shares
feed.summary: Wendy S. Bell, the Senior Executive VP and CFO of Carter Bankshares, Inc. (CARE), had 435 shares of common stock withheld on March 4, 2026, to cover tax obligations. The shares were valued at $20.98 per share. Following this transaction, Bell directly holds 40,223 shares of Carter Bankshares common stock.

feed.title: SelectQuote, Inc. (SLQT) Investigation: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman, LLC Encourages Stockholders to Contact the Firm to Learn More About the Investigation
feed.summary: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman, LLC is investigating potential claims on behalf of SelectQuote, Inc. (NYSE: SLQT) investors. The investigation focuses on whether SelectQuote and its officers engaged in corporate wrongdoing, specifically for those who purchased securities before September 9, 2020, and continue to hold them. The firm encourages affected stockholders to contact them to learn more and potentially assist in the investigation, offering their services on a contingency fee basis.

feed.title: Ex-Uber CEO Kalanick Debuts Plan for ‘Gainfully Employed Robots’
feed.summary: Former Uber CEO Travis Kalanick has launched a new venture named Atoms, which aims to create "gainfully employed robots" for various industries. This initiative involves transforming his real estate company, City Storage Systems, which operates CloudKitchens. Atoms will focus on robotics technology for food, mining, and automotive transport.

feed.title: Securities Fraud Investigation Into Insulet Corporation (PODD) Announced – Shareholders Who Lost Money Urged To Contact The Law Offices of Frank R. Cruz
feed.summary: The Law Offices of Frank R. Cruz have announced an investigation into Insulet Corporation (PODD) regarding potential violations of federal securities laws. This follows Insulet's disclosure of a manufacturing issue with specific lots of Omnipod® 5 Pods, which caused a drop in the company's stock price. Shareholders who incurred losses are encouraged to contact the law firm to explore pursuing a claim.

feed.title: Regulators sign off on utilities’ purchase of solar, battery project in Rock County
feed.summary: Wisconsin regulators have approved the acquisition of the Dawn Harvest Solar and Battery Energy Storage Facility in Rock County by We Energies, Wisconsin Public Service, and Madison Gas and Electric. This project, part of a larger $2 billion plan, will add 150 megawatts of solar power and 50 megawatts of battery storage to the grid, capable of powering over 45,000 homes. The utilities believe these renewable projects will lower costs for customers and help meet rising energy demands, including those from data centers.

feed.title: Coca-Cola: Insider Selling Could Be An Ominous Sign (NYSE:KO)
feed.summary: Despite improved fundamentals and positive growth projections, Coca-Cola's valuation remains elevated. Recent significant insider selling, including a major sale by the CEO, raises concerns about management's confidence and the stock's future prospects. The author reiterates a sell rating on Coca-Cola due to mixed near-term outlook, high valuation, and questionable upside, despite its long-term potential.

feed.title: Assessing UnitedHealth Group (UNH) Valuation After Conference Updates And Fresh Institutional Interest
feed.summary: UnitedHealth Group (UNH) is currently trading at US$277.05, significantly below a perceived fair value of $486.86, suggesting it might be undervalued. This assessment follows recent updates at the Barclays Global Healthcare Conference and increasing institutional interest, despite recent short-term share price declines. The valuation narrative is supported by UNH's growing premium base, contract wins, strong Optum business, solid financial position, and favorable industry trends like an aging population.

feed.title: Storms cut power to 396,000 customers in US Midwest, Mid-Atlantic; Ohio hardest hit
feed.summary: Severe storms across the U.S. Midwest and Mid-Atlantic regions have left over 396,000 homes and businesses without power. Ohio is the hardest-hit state, with approximately 123,000 outages, representing 2.3% of its customers. American Electric Power (AEP) is the most affected utility in Ohio, with about 40,000 of its customers experiencing outages.

feed.title: Waste Management (WM) Initiated with 'Peer Perform' by Wolfe Research | WM Stock News
feed.summary: Wolfe Research has initiated coverage on Waste Management (WM) with a 'Peer Perform' rating, though without a specific price target. This follows several other analysts who recently raised their price targets for WM while maintaining existing ratings, including Stifel, Wells Fargo, Oppenheimer, RBC Capital, and TD Cowen. Waste Management is North America's largest integrated provider of solid waste services and a leading recycler, operating numerous landfills and transfer stations.

feed.title: Verizon (NYSE: VZ) CFO awarded phantom stock units under deferred plan
feed.summary: Verizon's Executive Vice President and CFO, Anthony T. Skiadas, was granted 120.397 unitized phantom stock units on March 12, 2026, under a deferred compensation plan. These units are economically tied to Verizon common stock but are settled in cash, not actual shares. Following this transaction, Skiadas indirectly holds 141,222.990 phantom stock units, including those accumulated from dividend reinvestment.

feed.title: Convenience stores see momentum in nicotine category
feed.summary: Convenience stores like Casey's General Stores and Arko are experiencing significant growth in the nicotine category, particularly from alternative products such as pouches and vapor. These alternatives offer higher margin rates than traditional combustible cigarettes, contributing to overall margin improvement despite a continued decline in cigarette sales. The slowing rate of decline in combustible sales, coupled with the rise of these new products, suggests a potential return to positive growth for the entire nicotine category in the c-store sector.

feed.title: Verizon (VZ) EVP Kyle Malady receives new phantom stock award
feed.summary: Verizon EVP Kyle Malady was granted 120.397 unitized phantom stock awards, valued at $14.44 per unit, as part of a deferred compensation plan. These units are economically tied to Verizon common stock but are settled in cash upon pre-established events, not through open-market transactions. Following this grant and dividend reinvestment, Malady's deferred compensation account now holds 410,521.482 phantom stock units.

feed.title: HP Hit With Lawsuit Over ‘Misleading’ Cookie Consent Banners
feed.summary: HP Inc. is facing a lawsuit from six website users who allege the company secretly tracked their personal data despite user attempts to control data sharing. The lawsuit claims HP used tracking pixels from various tech companies, like TikTok, Meta, Google, Reddit, and X Corp., to intercept user communications even when visitors declined cookies or opted out. The complaint, filed in the US District Court for the Northern District of California, also asserts that HP displayed different cookie banners to users depending on their state.

feed.title: Exxon officers' compensation dips after rocky 2025, could revive in 2026 with skyrocketing stock price
feed.summary: Exxon Mobil's top officers experienced a slight dip in compensation from 2024 to 2025. However, their stock-based compensation is anticipated to significantly increase in 2026 if the company's stock price continues its upward trend. The article suggests a potential recovery in executive compensation following a challenging year.

feed.title: Church & Dwight (NYSE:CHD) Director Sells $704,060.00 in Stock
feed.summary: Church & Dwight (NYSE:CHD) Director Penry Price sold 7,000 shares worth $704,060.00 on March 12th, reducing his stake by almost 19%. The company recently reported quarterly results beating expectations with $0.86 EPS and $1.64B revenue, and increased its quarterly dividend to $0.3075. Several hedge funds have adjusted their positions in CHD, and analysts currently have a consensus "Hold" rating with an average price target of $101.88.

feed.title: Class I Briefs: CSX, NS, CN
feed.summary: CSX subsidiary Quality Carriers, Inc. has been named Unilever’s Bulk Carrier of the Year for 2025, and CSX is supporting HD Hyundai Power Transformers USA's $200 million expansion. Norfolk Southern’s Triple Crown Services received an intermodal honor from Averitt. CN's Halifax Intermodal team is celebrating 25 years with no injuries.

feed.title: Director John Hoke awarded 1,956 Target (TGT) shares in equity grant
feed.summary: Target Corporation director John R. Hoke III was granted 1,956 shares of common stock as part of an equity award on March 11, 2026. This transaction is classified as a compensation grant under the company's 2020 Long-Term Incentive Plan, with a zero-dollar share price. Following this grant, Hoke directly holds 1,956 shares, in addition to 50 shares indirectly held by his spouse.

feed.title: Target's new CEO cuts prices to win back shoppers, boost revenue
feed.summary: Target's new CEO, Michael Fiddelke, is implementing a strategy of significant price cuts on over 3,000 products, following a playbook used by his predecessor. This move aims to boost demand and reverse three years of declining sales, alongside a $6 billion investment in capital expenditures and operating expenses to improve stores, accelerate deliveries, and enhance technology. While analysts see this as a step in the right direction, they emphasize that price cuts alone may not be enough to regain market share from competitors like Walmart and Amazon, and sustained investor patience will be crucial.

feed.title: The FTC is mailing $47M in checks to renters—but many don’t realize they’re eligible
feed.summary: The FTC is distributing $47.2 million in checks to over 444,000 renters who leased homes from Invitation Homes. This settlement stems from a 2024 lawsuit alleging that the Dallas-based company charged undisclosed fees and mishandled security deposits. Eligible renters are those who paid $45 or more for certain fees between January 2021 and September 2024 and have not yet received a refund from the company.

feed.title: Quest Diagnostics Elects Wentworth to Board of Directors
feed.summary: Quest Diagnostics has elected Timothy Wentworth, a highly experienced business leader, to its board of directors, increasing the board to eleven members. Wentworth previously served as CEO of Walgreens Boots Alliance and was the founding CEO of Evernorth Health Services, bringing extensive healthcare, operational, and HR expertise to Quest. His appointment is expected to provide valuable leadership as Quest Diagnostics continues its mission to create a healthier world.

feed.title: Adobe Settles With U.S. Over Hard-to-Cancel Subscriptions
feed.summary: Adobe has agreed to pay $75 million to the U.S. Justice Department and provide $75 million in free services to customers to settle a lawsuit. The suit accused the company of making it difficult for users to cancel subscriptions to products like Photoshop and hiding expensive cancellation fees. This settlement is part of a broader trend by the Trump administration to resolve consumer protection lawsuits initiated under the Biden administration.

feed.title: Match Group’s Tinder push builds momentum but analysts remain cautious
feed.summary: Analysts at Jefferies maintained a 'Hold' rating on Match Group Inc (NASDAQ:MTCH) despite an acceleration in product development for its Tinder app. While new features like Music Mode, Astrology Mode, live interaction formats, and AI-driven matching are encouraging, structural concerns about the online dating category persist. These new developments aim to deepen user engagement and expand reasons for matching beyond physical appearance, while also improving trust and safety.

feed.title: Baxter’s new CEO paid $29.8 million for less than five months of work in 2025
feed.summary: Andrew Hider, the new CEO of Baxter, received a compensation package of nearly $30 million for less than five months of work in 2025. This significant payment highlights the substantial remuneration sometimes given to top executives upon joining a company. The article, published by Crain's Chicago Business, signals a focus on high-level executive compensation within major corporations.

feed.title: Core Molding (CMT) EVP gains shares via performance awards
feed.summary: Michael James Gayford, EVP of Operations at Core Molding Technologies (CMT), acquired 4,846 shares and an additional 314 shares through the vesting and settlement of 2023 performance share awards on March 11, 2026. These transactions were stock-based compensation and not open-market purchases. Following these awards, Gayford directly holds 26,667 common shares in the company.

feed.title: AI Push and Global Expansion Might Change The Case For Investing In Chefs' Warehouse (CHEF)
feed.summary: The Chefs’ Warehouse (CHEF) is emphasizing AI and digital tools, alongside facility expansions and integration of Hardie's, to improve sales and operations. While these efforts are expected to support execution, the primary investment narrative still hinges on managing acquisition complexity and successfully integrating new businesses like Hardie's. Analysts project significant revenue and earnings growth by 2028, leading to a fair value estimate with potential upside.

feed.title: Insider Sell: Penry Price Sells 7,000 Shares of Church & Dwight Co Inc (CHD)
feed.summary: Penry Price, a Director at Church & Dwight Co Inc (CHD), sold 7,000 shares of the company on March 12, 2026, reducing his holding to 860 shares. Over the past year, Price has sold 21,660 shares, with the company seeing 5 insider buys and 7 insider sells. Despite the sale, the stock is considered fairly valued at $100.58, with a price-to-GF-Value ratio of 0.95.

feed.title: McDonald's Expands Value Menu With $3 Items
feed.summary: McDonald's is set to launch a new value menu with items priced at $3 and under this April in the U.S. This initiative aims to attract price-conscious customers and strengthen its competitive position, responding to a wider industry trend of introducing lower-priced options. Analysts suggest that these aggressive bargain offerings could impact industry pricing practices and help McDonald's maintain customer traffic.

feed.title: Is Mid-America Apartment Communities (MAA) Attractive After A 17.6% One-Year Share Price Decline?
feed.summary: Mid-America Apartment Communities (MAA) has seen a 17.6% share price decline over the past year, prompting an analysis of its valuation. A Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests the stock is undervalued by 34.5%, with an estimated intrinsic value of $195.03 per share compared to its current price of $127.75. However, its P/E ratio of 33.71x is higher than the industry average and the Simply Wall St Fair Ratio, indicating it might be overvalued by this metric.

feed.title: Intuitive Surgical Stock Falls After Cybersecurity Breach Hits Internal Systems
feed.summary: Intuitive Surgical (ISRG) shares fell 2% after the company disclosed a cybersecurity breach affecting some internal IT applications due to a targeted phishing incident. Despite the breach, Intuitive Surgical emphasized that its core products and operations remain fully functional. This incident highlights a growing trend of cyberattacks within the medical technology sector.

feed.title: General Dynamics (NYSE: GD) CEO sells 32,918 shares, holds 766,457
feed.summary: General Dynamics CEO Phebe N. Novakovic reported selling 32,918 shares of the company's common stock on March 11, 2026, through open-market transactions at prices ranging from approximately $353.75 to $354.24 per share. Following these sales, she directly holds 766,457 shares and indirectly holds additional shares through a 401(k) plan and a limited liability company. The Form 4 filing details these transactions and her remaining beneficial ownership.

feed.title: Quantitative Investment Management Boosts Stake in Cooper Companies
feed.summary: Quantitative Investment Management LLC significantly increased its stake in The Cooper Companies, Inc. by 111.8% in the third quarter, bringing its total holdings to 36,024 shares valued at $2,469,000. This increase suggests the institutional investor's confidence in the medical device company's performance and growth prospects. Cooper Companies is a key player in vision care and women's health markets, making institutional ownership changes a strong indicator of market sentiment.

feed.title: Costco Says Any Tariff Refunds Will Go Back to Customers Through Lower Prices
feed.summary: Costco announced that any tariff refunds it receives will be passed directly to customers through lower prices, a practice consistent with its past actions. CEO Ron Vachris stated the company will be transparent about how these savings are returned. This decision follows a U.S. Supreme Court ruling against Trump-era tariffs and Costco's own lawsuit seeking refunds.

feed.title: Ag Groups Urge Mosaic, J.R. Simplot to Renounce Fertilizer Duties
feed.summary: A coalition of 64 agricultural groups, led by the National Corn Growers Association, has called on Mosaic Company and J.R. Simplot to advocate for the removal of duties on imported phosphate products from Morocco. Citing the impact of the Middle East conflict and already high U.S. input prices, the groups argue that these duties contribute to increased fertilizer costs and shortages, negatively affecting U.S. agricultural security. The duties were initially imposed in 2020 following a petition by Mosaic, which was supported by J.R. Simplot, alleging unfair foreign subsidies.

feed.title: Applied Materials Lifts Dividend As AI Partnerships Shape Shareholder Returns
feed.summary: Applied Materials (AMAT) has increased its quarterly cash dividend by 15%, marking nine years of dividend growth, while simultaneously expanding partnerships with Micron and SK Hynix for AI memory development. The company, trading at $337.27 with significant year-to-date and annual returns, is balancing shareholder returns with substantial R&D investments in AI-focused chip production, though insider selling has been noted as a potential risk.

feed.title: Assessing BlackLine (BL) Valuation After Recent Share Price Weakness And Mixed Profitability Signals
feed.summary: BlackLine (BL) has experienced significant share price weakness, falling 20% in the last month and 39% in the past three months, despite annual revenue growth of 9.8% and net income growth of 48.8%. While a popular narrative suggests the stock is 19.2% undervalued with a fair value of $44.00, its high P/E ratio of 86.3x compared to the industry average of 28.1x points to potential valuation risk. Investors are encouraged to review the fundamentals and consider potential risks or opportunities, such as platform traction or deepening partnerships.

feed.title: Is Huntington Bancshares (HBAN) Attractively Priced After Recent Share Price Weakness
feed.summary: Huntington Bancshares (HBAN) has experienced recent share price weakness, with a 7.2% decline in the past week. Despite this, an Excess Returns analysis suggests the stock is undervalued by 57.2%, estimating an intrinsic value of $36.20 per share compared to its current price of $15.48. Its P/E ratio of 15.06x is slightly above the industry average but aligns closely with Simply Wall St’s Fair Ratio, indicating it is broadly priced correctly by that measure.

feed.title: A Look At Wynn Resorts (WYNN) Valuation As Middle East Tensions Test UAE Project Prospects
feed.summary: Wynn Resorts' valuation is under scrutiny due to Middle East tensions impacting its UAE project, Wynn Al Marjan Island. The stock has seen a recent decline despite a positive 1-year return, with analysts suggesting it is undervalued by 30.5% due to the potential of its resort pipeline, though a high P/E ratio indicates some valuation risk. The article advises investors to review the numbers and consider risks like Macau regulation or the UAE project underperforming.

feed.title: BofA, Evercore, and Morgan Stanley Cut Price Targets for HP Inc. (HPQ)
feed.summary: BofA, Evercore ISI, and Morgan Stanley have all cut their price targets for HP Inc. (HPQ) following the company's recent earnings results. Analysts cited factors like slower PC unit growth, margin pressure from memory costs, leadership transition uncertainty, and a tough memory environment for their revised outlooks. Despite Morgan Stanley seeing the stock as "cheap," they forecast over 14% downside risk to fiscal year 2026 EPS guidance.

feed.title: Advanced Micro Devices, Inc. (AMD) Focused on Growth through Strategic Partnerships
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices, Inc. (AMD) is prioritizing growth through strategic partnerships, particularly in the data center and AI markets. CEO Lisa Su highlighted this focus at a recent conference, while the company has already secured significant partnerships with Meta Platforms and OpenAI to power AI infrastructure. AMD aims for substantial AI revenue and a high compound annual growth rate in the coming years.

feed.title: Pawtucket to hold job fair for Hasbro workers, despite not knowing how many jobs will be lost
feed.summary: The city of Pawtucket plans to organize a job fair for Hasbro Inc. employees in response to the company's relocation to Boston. This initiative is moving forward despite the city not knowing the exact number of Rhode Island workers who will be displaced by the move. Hasbro has already shed 1,970 jobs globally since 2022, but has not yet informed the state about layoffs specifically tied to the Boston relocation.

feed.title: UBS and BofA Lift Price Targets on Viatris (VTRS)
feed.summary: UBS and BofA have both raised their price targets on Viatris (VTRS) following the company's cost-saving initiatives and favorable 2026 guidance. UBS maintained a Buy rating, citing expected cost-saving targets and potential for long-term growth and EPS increase, while BofA maintained an Underperform rating despite the increased price target. Viatris plans to achieve $400 million in net cost reductions over three years.

feed.title: Intuitive Surgical discloses cybersecurity breach
feed.summary: Intuitive Surgical disclosed a cybersecurity breach where an unauthorized third party accessed internal IT business applications due to a phishing incident. The breach compromised some customer business and contact information, as well as employee and corporate data, but did not affect its robotic surgical systems or manufacturing operations. The company is investigating the incident and has emphasized that its robotic systems have separate security protocols.

feed.title: Campbell Soup Co. Faces Financial Challenges Amid Valuation Concerns and Bearish Trends
feed.summary: Campbell Soup Co. is facing significant financial challenges due to shifts in its evaluation, reflecting both technical and valuation concerns. The company is experiencing a bearish trend, with indicators like MACD, Bollinger Bands, and moving averages signaling downward movement. Furthermore, Campbell Soup Co. is classified as very expensive compared to industry peers, with a high P/E ratio and enterprise value to EBITDA, raising significant valuation concerns despite decent profitability metrics.

feed.title: Insulet to spend up to $40M on Omnipod 5 pod recall - Boston Business Journal
feed.summary: Insulet Corp. is set to incur costs of up to $40 million for the recall of its Omnipod 5 tubeless insulin pump pods. The Acton-based company initiated the recall after receiving 18 reports of serious adverse events related to insufficient insulin delivery from the devices.

feed.title: American Express Sues Ray J Over $78,000 in Unpaid Credit Card Bills
feed.summary: American Express has filed a lawsuit against singer Ray J for $78,704.56 in unpaid credit card bills, alleging a breach of contract. This comes after a previous lawsuit by AmEx in November for even larger unpaid bills. Ray J is also involved in a separate and ongoing legal battle with Kim Kardashian and Kris Jenner concerning his 2007 sex tape.

feed.title: Fifth Third Bancorp (FITB) Target Price Lowered by Truist Securi
feed.summary: Truist Securities has lowered its price target for Fifth Third Bancorp (FITB) from $60.00 to $53.00, while maintaining a "Buy" rating. This adjustment comes despite other analysts raising their price targets for the regional bank in recent months. The average one-year price target from 19 analysts is $57.61, suggesting a potential upside of 30.82% from its current price.

feed.title: International Paper to Sell its Global Cellulose Fibers Business
feed.summary: International Paper announced its definitive agreement to sell its Global Cellulose Fibers (GCF) business unit to American Industrial Partners for approximately $1.5 billion. This strategic move aims to sharpen the company's focus on its core operations and expanding sustainable packaging solutions. The GCF business, which generated $2.8 billion in 2024 revenue, produces fluff pulp for hygiene products and specialty pulp for industrial applications.

feed.title: SLB (SLB) Is Down 6.0% After Middle East Disruptions Cut Q1 Outlook Amid New Malaysia Win
feed.summary: SLB (SLB) experienced a 6.0% drop after announcing that first-quarter revenue would be below expectations due to Middle East disruptions, impacting earnings by US$0.06–US$0.09 per diluted share. Concurrently, its OneSubsea joint venture secured a significant deepwater subsea contract in Malaysia, highlighting the company's ability to win new projects in other regions despite geopolitical shocks. This dual development creates a nuanced investment narrative, requiring investors to weigh regional instability against new growth opportunities and long-term forecasts.
